description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a symmetrical front facade, featuring a covered porch supported by white columns and decorative railings. The roof is flat with two prominent brick chimneys and a patterned shingle design on the front gable. The front porch is elevated with a set of steps leading up to the main entrance, which is flanked by windows on both sides. The side walls are plain red brick with regularly spaced rectangular windows, and the rear of the house is simpler, lacking the ornate details of the front. The house exhibits classic early 20th-century American residential architecture, with a focus on symmetry and modest decorative elements. The overall structure is robust, with a solid brick exterior and traditional wooden trim.
description: A two-story brick house with a flat roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. Decorative white trim and columns accent the front facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate porch, white columns, patterned steps, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying plain brick walls, two rectangular windows, and a small window near the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simple facade, two windows, and a less decorative porch area.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with plain brickwork and window placement.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with steps, covered porch, white columns, decorative railings, and patterned tilework.
